_comment: SC-001a / SC-002 baseline â€” manually captured, never auto-updated. Re-capture requires a reviewed commit.

reference_machine.cpu: x86_64 GitHub Actions hosted runner (ubuntu-latest, 4-core). See git log for exact capture commit.
reference_machine._dev_capture_note: PR 4b-D.2 commit-8 lint_10kb capture was on dev WSL2, NOT ubuntu-latest â€” see `lint_10kb._wsl2_dev_capture` sub-object below. WSL2 typically reads 5-10% slower than GHA ubuntu-latest. The first GHA re-capture of this branch via `scripts/capture-baselines.sh` becomes the new authoritative `lint_10kb.{lower,mean,upper}_ci_us`; the WSL2 numbers stay in the sub-object as a reference point for cross-host comparisons. Other baselines in this file (`decoder_10kb_one_mangled_region`, etc.) are still GHA captures from 2026-05-17 (PR #498).

_wsl2_dev_capture._note: Authoritative `lint_10kb.{lower,mean,upper}_ci_us` ABOVE currently hold the WSL2 dev-worktree numbers as a stop-gap. The first GHA `ubuntu-latest` re-capture of this branch via `scripts/capture-baselines.sh` overwrites the top-level numbers; this sub-object preserves the WSL2 measurement as a reference point. Expected GHA delta: 50-100Âµs lower (5-10% per the reference-machine note).

lint_10kb._note: Values are Criterion 95% confidence-interval bounds. **2026-05-17 PR 4b-D.2 hot-path-flip re-capture.** Measured median 1033Âµs on dev WSL2 worktree post-commit-8; the GHA `ubuntu-latest` runner typically reads 5-10% faster than WSL2 per the reference-machine note below. The PR 4b-D.2 flip moves the page-marking projection from `PageContext::project()` to `scheme.project(Scope::Page, ...) â†’ join_via_lattice â†’ closure â†’ page_rewrites`; the new pipeline does ~50% more O(n) walks per call than the pre-flip path (lattice constructors for 10 axes + closure + page rewrites vs PageContext's 13 expected_* accessors), but PageContext stays alive as the page-state accumulator (PR 4b-E deletes it). Commits 6-8 brought the regression from +65% (unmitigated) to +13% via three optimizations: (6) closure short-circuit on empty cone triggers (architect's R-1); (7) engine-side fast-path `project_from_attrs_slice` skipping the trait-path's redundant `m.0.clone()` round; (8) `project_from_page_context` borrowing the engine's existing PageContext through to `join_via_lattice_with_context`, eliminating the inner tmp_ctx rebuild's nÃ—clone per call. The new threshold (`upper_ci_us * 1.10` â‰ˆ 1140Âµs) accommodates the structural cost of the new pipeline + the GHA-vs-WSL2 measurement difference; expect the GHA re-capture to land 50-100Âµs lower than this WSL2 value once CI runs. Per the `project_perf_baseline_pr5_trigger.md` project memory, further perf-analysis work is scheduled for PR 5+ if structural costs survive; PR 4b-E (PageContext deletion) will retire the remaining residue-axis tmp_ctx requirement, expected to bring the value back down. **Previous 2026-05-17 re-capture (PR #498) â€” superseded.** Previous values (lower 912 / mean 913 / upper 914) were captured pre-PR-4b-D.2; the flip's structural-cost ceiling makes them no longer reachable on the new pipeline. The SC-001 16ms absolute target remains the load-bearing constitution gate; the re-capture is invisible to the SC-001 ceiling. **2026-05-05 widening (D8 amendment) â€” superseded.** Original 2026-04-26 baseline (lower 746 / mean 749 / upper 753) flapped on shared GHA `ubuntu-latest` runners; per D8 `decisions.md`, the +10% widening (746â†’821, 749â†’824, 753â†’828) held until staging complexity additions exceeded the noise band.
lint_10kb._p99_note: T098 / FR-030 / SC-008 tail-percentile gate, **partial activation**. Pins p99 cost of the `Arc<dyn Vocabulary<S>>::shape_admits` indirect call introduced in PR 2; vtable misses surface at the tail, not in the mean. **Status as of 2026-05-05:** only the absolute gate (`target_p99_us = 16000Âµs`, the SC-001 16ms constitutional ceiling applied to p99 specifically) is wired here. The drift gate (`p99_baseline * 1.05` per FR-030 / SC-008 `p99 â‰¤ baseline + 5%`) requires a `p99_us` baseline captured on the same hardware as the existing `upper_ci_us` baseline (`ubuntu-latest` per `reference_machine.profile`); a WSL2 dev-worktree capture would turn the drift gate into runner-noise flake on shared GHA runners. **Re-activation procedure (follow-up):** capture `p99_us` on `ubuntu-latest` via `scripts/capture-baselines.sh` (using `compute_percentiles_python` for capture-time / gate-time agreement), add the field to this block, and the drift check in `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_one_bench` will activate automatically (the gate is field-presence-gated). The absolute gate stays load-bearing across both states.

decoder_10kb_one_mangled_region._note: Engine::lint with `with_deep_scan()` on a 10KB document containing exactly one mangled portion (`(SERCET//NF)`) plus normal valid markings + prose. `SERCET` is the deliberate misspelling the SC-002 bench feeds the engine to force the decoder to fire â€” it is NOT a typo to be corrected. **2026-05-17 re-capture (PR #498).** Measured upper CI landed at 1158Âµs on `ubuntu-latest` GHA runner (PR #498 CI run 25991729674), 46Âµs over the previous 1113Âµs threshold â€” a substantive shift, not noise. Refreshed lower/mean/upper to the new capture (1155 / 1157 / 1158); the new threshold (`upper_ci_us * 1.10` â‰ˆ 1274Âµs) restores headroom against intervening staging merges (PR 4b-B/C lattice landings, decoder priors work in #258/#259/#262, parser/recognizer additions). PR 4b-D's hot-path flip is expected to retire the old PageContext machinery on the decoder path and bring this value back down, at which point a routine re-capture steps the baseline back down. **2026-05-05 widening (D8 amendment) â€” superseded by this re-capture.** Original 2026-04-26 capture was lower 917 / mean 918 / upper 919; bumped uniformly +10% (917â†’1009, 918â†’1010, 919â†’1011) paired with the lint_10kb widening. The widening held until intervening complexity additions exceeded it. The SC-002 18ms absolute target remains the load-bearing constitution gate.

lint_scaling._note: SC-005 linear-scaling gate. RÂ² is the coefficient of determination from an ordinary-least-squares fit of (input_size_bytes, mean_time_Âµs) across the input-size sweep in `crates/engine/benches/linear_scaling.rs` (currently 1KB â†’ 100KB across seven points). 0.9 is the floor â€” substantially-better RÂ² is normal for the strict path because per-document work is dominated by linear scanner + parser passes; a value below 0.9 usually indicates either super-linear behavior (a new quadratic somewhere) or pathological noise from the runner. Enforced by `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_linear_scaling`. Choice of input-size scaling vs. worker-count scaling is documented in the script header.

fix_throughput._note: Linear-scaling gate for Engine::fix FixMode::Apply. RÂ² is the coefficient of determination from an OLS fit of (input_size_bytes, mean_time_Âµs) across the fix_throughput size sweep (1 MB â†’ 100 MB, five points). 0.9 is the floor â€” mirroring the lint_scaling gate. A value below 0.9 indicates super-linear (e.g. quadratic) behavior in the apply path, which guards the Vec::splice-per-fix regression. Enforced by scripts/bench-check.sh::check_fix_throughput.

deadline_overhead._note: Spec 005 T018/T019. Compares `deadline_overhead_baseline` (Engine::lint, no deadline) against `deadline_overhead_with_deadline` (Engine::lint_with_options with a 1-hour deadline that never trips). The ratio is computed from Criterion's mean (middle CI value), not the upper-CI bound â€” clock jitter inflates CI width and would convert noise into false positives on a tight ratio gate. The gate is set at 10% (permissive starting point) rather than the 2% design target from `specs/005-engine-deadlines/spec.md` because the per-candidate Instant::now() cost varies dramatically by host: native Linux x86 vDSO clock_gettime is ~10â€“30ns and the spec's â‰¤2% target is reachable, but WSL2's virtualized HV clock is ~500â€“700ns and observed end-to-end overhead is 7â€“12% on the same fixture. Tightening this gate to 2% is a follow-up once a CI-runner baseline is captured (mirrors the `MARQUE_BENCH_SKIP_REGRESSION` pattern for the +10% absolute regression gate). Enforced by `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_deadline_overhead`.

fix_10kb_pass2_only._note: PR 7c (T085) â€” SC-001 absolute target for the two-pass fix pipeline's pass-2-only path. Exercises the no-reshape short-circuit: pass-1 produces zero applied fixes, the pre-pass-1 cache stays empty, the re-parse arm short-circuits, and pass-2 dispatches against the post-pass-0 buffer. No `upper_ci_us` baseline captured here â€” capture and add to enable the +10% regression gate; the absolute SC-001 16ms ceiling stays load-bearing in the meantime. Capture procedure: run `cargo bench --bench fix_10kb -- --save-baseline pr7c` on this branch and on `origin/staging` (pre-pr7c), then add the `upper_ci_us` / `p99_us` fields. FR-033 delta gate (p99 â‰¤ baseline Ã— 1.05) activates automatically once `p99_us` lands. See `crates/engine/benches/fix_10kb.rs`.

fix_10kb_two_pass._note: PR 7c (T085) â€” SC-001 absolute target for the two-pass fix pipeline's full path. Exercises pass-0 corrections, pass-1 Localized fixes (C001 typo correction), the pre-pass-1 attrs cache population, post-pass-1 re-lint via `lint_with_options_internal_with_cache`, FR-023 disambiguation, and I-18 overlap demotion. (The originally-planned `PrecedingFixPenalty` application at the pass-2 threshold gate was retired in this same PR per D-7.22; the bench no longer exercises any penalty-application codepath.) As with `fix_10kb_pass2_only`, no `upper_ci_us` baseline is captured here â€” operator runs `cargo bench --bench fix_10kb -- --save-baseline pr7c` to populate. The SC-001 16ms absolute target is the load-bearing gate. See `crates/engine/benches/fix_10kb.rs`.
